title: Limonium otolepis
intro: Limonium otolepis, also known as sea lavender or sea rosemary, is a type of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Limonium otolepis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Limonium otolepis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
taxonomy: Limonium otolepis is a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Limonium, commonly known as sea lavenders. Limonium otolepis is further classified as a member of the order Plumbaginales, a group of flowering plants that are mainly coastal or saline. The family Plumbaginaceae, to which Limonium otolepis belongs, is a group of flowering plants that are often found in coastal areas or in environments with high salinity. Limonium otolepis is a specific species within this broader classification.
